meta.title: THE RESCUE — LIFELINE CUT V6 FINAL

event3.text: The pod beacon is ahead, but it keeps sliding in and out of the relay map like it doesn't want to be found.

event5.text: Life support is dropping. I can see the pulse in the telemetry.

event8.text: I heard Noah's voice in the last transmission. Or something that knew exactly how to hurt me.

event11.text: Nobody touches that hatch until we know what is moving inside it.

event13.text: That's easy for the dead engineer to say.

event3.text: Good. Technical work. Cables, relays, math. Things that don't pretend to be dead friends.

event5.text: I'm holding position while Hale filters the drifting pod signal.

event7.text: I need to keep the relay aligned. If I move, the beacon starts lying again.

event3.text: The pod is still moving, but at least now it's lying in a straight line.

event5.text: Signal is cleaner. Not safe. Cleaner.

event7.text: I hate that I can hear it better now.

event3.text: Fast route to visual range. I know you're going to hate this, Hale.

event10.text: I'm clipping to the rail and pushing through before the map shifts again.

event2.text: I have partial visual on the pod.

event4.text: It is closer than the map said. That should not be possible.

event6.text: The beacon dragged across my display while I moved, like the pod was correcting where I thought it was.

event8.text: So either the map is wrong, or the pod moved toward me.

event0.title: RESCUE ROUTE MAP
event0.content:
ROUTE A: Short route through exposed radiation corridor. Arrival fast. Crew risk severe. Pod risk moderate.

ROUTE B: Long route through maintenance spine. Arrival delayed. Crew risk moderate. Pod risk severe.
event0.caption: Recovered rescue route analysis. Both routes carry consequences.
event0.message: [RESCUE ROUTE MAP ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event2.text: Two paths. Neither one is good.

event4.text: Route A gets me there fast, but I take the radiation hit.

event6.text: Route B keeps me safer, but the pod loses time.

event8.text: Pick the sin you can live with, Operator.

event3.text: Radiation corridor ahead. The warning lights are dead, which feels rude.

event5.text: Do not stop in the exposed section.

event7.text: I was not planning a picnic, Ethan.

event10.text: Going quiet. I need to run this clean.

event4.text: My suit alarm is being very rude about my life choices.

event6.text: The corridor hummed through my teeth. I didn't know that was a thing a place could do.

event8.text: But I'm closer. If my hair falls out, I'm blaming you. Emotionally. Not legally.

event3.text: Safer for me. Worse for the clock.

event7.text: Sensible is a nice word for slow when someone is suffocating.

event10.text: I need to crawl most of this. Don't expect charm from me when I get back.

event3.text: I found older boot marks in the dust. Someone used this route before us.

event5.text: One set stops halfway and turns back. That's comforting in exactly no way.

event7.text: I got here safer. The pod lost time. Both facts are sitting in my chest.

event3.text: The hull is frosted from the inside.

event5.text: Noah used to draw faces in condensation on viewport glass. Terrible faces. Like potatoes with opinions.

event9.title: POD EXTERIOR SCAN
event9.content:
Hull Temperature: below operational range
Seal Pressure: unstable
Interior Motion: intermittent
Audio Contamination: rising
Manual Override: possible
Warning: signal distortion resembles recursive voice fold
event9.caption: Exterior scan of the pod. The pod is physically reachable but unstable.
event9.message: [POD EXTERIOR SCAN ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

choice0.text: SECURE THE POD BEFORE TOUCHING THE HATCH.

event3.text: Anchoring line is out. If it moves, I move with it instead of under it.

event5.text: That is somehow both reassuring and horrifying.

event3.text: The pod stopped fighting the drift.

event5.text: I wish that sentence felt less alive.

event7.text: The line vibrates every few seconds, like something inside is answering the beacon.

event3.text: The pod is still drifting. I see it.

event5.text: Maya, back off. The pod is still moving.

event8.text: I need one hand on the rail and one on the hatch frame.

event4.text: I hit the rail. I'm fine.

event6.text: That was a lie. My shoulder disagrees, but it's still attached.

event8.text: The hatch is reachable, but the whole frame is carrying motion through it.

event1.text: I need to tell you something before you touch the override.

event3.text: I have seen this pod before.

event7.text: No body. No suit. No blood. Just frost on the inside of the glass.

event13.text: That is such an Ethan answer.

event0.title: OLD POD ACCESS LOG
event0.content:
Access Event: Emergency Pod Manual Inspection
Timestamp: Before Incident Alpha
Operator: Ethan Hale
Pod State: empty
Interior Residue: frost / no biological matter detected
Seal Reclosure: completed manually
Current Conflict: active motion and biometrics now detected inside same pod
event0.caption: Recovered old access log. Hale previously opened the pod before Alpha.
event0.message: [OLD POD ACCESS LOG ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: I hate when a bad answer becomes a documented bad answer.

event10.text: Or something was always there and the system learned not to see it.

event3.text: I know Hale wants records. I know records matter.

event5.text: But the hatch is real and the clock is real and whatever is breathing in there is real enough for the pod to keep it alive.

backgroundEvent6.text: Reality is exactly what this station edits best.

backgroundEvent7.text: Then keep talking while I work.

event9.text: I am exposing the manual override now.

event3.text: It looks old enough to hate me personally.

event5.text: The hatch clicked twice while I was working. Not metal settling. A deliberate sound.

event0.title: POD ACCESS RISK REPORT
event0.content:
Interior Motion: detected
Seal Pressure: unstable
Manual Override: required
Audio Channel Contamination: rising
External Voice Traffic: unauthorized
Recommendation: choose access protocol before opening
event0.caption: Recovered access risk report. The pod can be opened, but the audio channel is contaminated.
event0.message: [POD ACCESS RISK REPORT ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

event4.text: The pod keeps making little pressure clicks. Like fingernails against glass.

event7.text: Operator. Decide how I open it.

event9.text: Careful means slower. Fast means I may not control what comes out.

event3.text: That buys control.

event8.text: I'm bleeding pressure from the pod seal. I need to keep the valve steady.

event3.text: The voice got weaker during venting.

event5.text: I don't know if that is good or monstrous.

event7.text: The hatch is safer now. The thing inside sounds less human.

event3.text: Maya, no—

event8.text: I'm bypassing pressure sequencing and pulling the release.

event4.text: Something hit the inside of the hatch.

event6.text: I heard it through my gloves.

event8.text: The pod wants open now. I can feel it fighting the latch.

event3.text: If you open it, you commit to whatever is inside.

event5.text: If you abort, you may leave a person to die.

event9.text: Good. That means you understand them.

event3.text: I have internal air mixing.

event5.text: Keep your face away from the seam.

event10.text: I'm releasing the final hatch lock.

event2.text: The hatch is open enough to see a glove.

event6.text: If this is a trap, it knows exactly which dead man to wear.

event8.text: He's breathing. Or the pod is making him look like he is.

event4.text: I hate you a little right now.

event6.text: Not forever. Maybe not even fairly. But right now.

backgroundEvent8.text: Maya, step away from the hatch.

event12.text: I need to pull away before the beacon talks me back into this.

event3.text: The beacon is still following us.

event5.text: That feels like an answer we didn't ask for.

event7.text: I can still hear it clicking through the line. One tap. Two taps. Like Noah used to do.
